ncs_standard_definition.description_short: A Veterinary Officer / Surgeon is responsible for the health and well-being of animals, providing medical care, vaccinations, and performing surgeries.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Veterinary Officers / Surgeons work in clinics, hospitals, or research institutions, diagnosing and treating animal diseases, performing surgeries, and providing preventative care. They may also be involved in research, education, and public health initiatives.

first_stepper.description: Pursue a degree in Animal Husbandry or a related field after completing high school.

choice_maker.description: If you have a generic degree, consider a bridge course in Animal Husbandry or a related field.

future_builder.description: If you are already working in the veterinary sector, consider advancing your skills by learning specialized techniques or taking on leadership roles.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2 years as a Veterinary Assistant or Junior Veterinary Officer

problem_solver_uturn.description: If you are switching from an unrelated field, focus on developing relevant skills and building a portfolio of work experience.
portfolio_proof_required: Volunteer experience at an animal shelter
